segments.caption: The video opens with a wide shot of the Men's High Jump Final at the Rio 2016 Olympics, set in a large, brightly lit stadium at night. The track is blue, and the high jump apparatus is centered, with a group of drummers in red and white uniforms standing near the landing mat, adding a festive atmosphere. The camera pans across the scene, showing officials, photographers, and athletes preparing. On-screen graphics display the event title and Olympic rings. The view shifts to a closer angle of the high jump setup, where a man in a gray hoodie walks near the mat. The graphics then transition to show the world record (2.45m by Javier Sotomayor, Cuba, 1993) and Olympic record (2.39m by Charles Austin, USA, 1996). The start list for the final is displayed, listing 15 athletes from various nations including Bulgaria, Australia, Ukraine, Cyprus, Syria, USA, Canada, and Great Britain. The camera then cuts to close-ups of athletes: first, Erik Kynard of the USA in a blue jacket, covering his mouth in a gesture of focus or anticipation, followed by Derek Drouin of Canada in a red jacket, smiling confidently. The final shot shows Derek Drouin in his competition uniform—black and red with 'CANADA' and 'DROUIN' on the front—walking along the track, preparing for his jump, with spectators and flags visible in the background.

discovered_new_entities.Erik Kynard: Athlete from USA wearing a blue Nike jacket with the American flag patch, seen covering his mouth with his hand in a focused gesture.
discovered_new_entities.Derek Drouin: Athlete from Canada, first seen in a red Nike jacket with a Canadian flag patch, smiling confidently; later shown in his black and red competition uniform with 'CANADA' and 'DROUIN' printed on the front, walking along the track.
discovered_new_entities.High Jump Apparatus: Green-poled high jump setup with a white bar, positioned over a blue landing mat, central to the competition area.
discovered_new_entities.Drummers: Group of performers in red and white uniforms, standing near the landing mat, adding ceremonial music to the event atmosphere.
discovered_new_entities.Start List Graphics: On-screen display listing 15 athletes competing in the Men's High Jump Final, including names, nationalities, and flags, shown in two segments.
discovered_new_entities.World and Olympic Record Graphics: On-screen overlay showing the world record (2.45m by Javier Sotomayor, 1993) and Olympic record (2.39m by Charles Austin, 1996) for the event.
discovered_new_entities.Stadium Environment: Large Olympic stadium at night, illuminated by bright overhead lights, with blue seating, spectators, and Rio 2016 branding visible around the track.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a close-up of Derek Drouin, the Canadian high jumper, wearing his black and red competition uniform with 'CANADA' and 'DROUIN' printed on the front, along with a small Canadian flag and Nike logo. He appears focused and composed as he prepares for his first attempt at 2.20m, as indicated by the on-screen graphic. The camera follows him as he begins his run-up along the blue track, accelerating toward the High Jump Apparatus. He executes a powerful Fosbury Flop, clearing the white bar with precision and landing safely on the blue mat. The crowd in the Stadium Environment, illuminated by bright overhead lights, watches intently. After the successful jump, the camera captures his reaction—calm and confident—as he walks away from the mat. The scene then shifts to another athlete, wearing a red uniform, who attempts the same height but knocks the bar off with his back, resulting in a failed attempt. This athlete, visibly disappointed, walks away from the mat as officials raise a red flag. The segment concludes with a wide shot of the competition area, showing the High Jump Apparatus, the landing mat, and the surrounding environment, including seated officials and equipment, with the Rio 2016 branding visible on the mat and surrounding structures.

segments.caption: The video segment captures a high jump competition at the Rio 2016 Olympics, showcasing three athletes attempting the 2.20m bar. First, an athlete in a red uniform with 'QATAR' and 'AL-MAZROUI' on his bib runs and attempts the jump but knocks the bar off with his back, failing the attempt; he walks away with a disappointed expression. Next, Bohdan Bondarenko of Ukraine, wearing a blue and yellow uniform with 'UKRAINE' and 'BONDARENKO' on his bib, executes a clean Fosbury Flop, clearing the bar successfully and landing on the blue mat with a satisfied gesture. Finally, Derek Drouin of Canada, in a black and red uniform with 'CANADA' and 'DROUIN' printed on the front, prepares with focus, runs, and clears the 2.20m bar with precision, landing confidently and walking away with a composed demeanor. The stadium is brightly lit at night, filled with spectators, and features Rio 2016 branding on the landing mat and track. On-screen graphics display each athlete’s name, country, and attempt height (2.20m), with the Olympic rings visible. The green-poled high jump apparatus remains central throughout, and the atmosphere is intense and competitive.

segments.caption: The video segment captures a high jump competition at the Rio 2016 Olympics, set in a large, brightly lit stadium filled with spectators. The sequence begins with Derek Drouin of Canada, wearing a black and red uniform with 'CANADA' and 'DROUIN' printed on the front, preparing for his jump. He executes a successful Fosbury Flop over the 2.20m bar, landing cleanly on the blue mat, and walks away with a composed expression. The camera then shifts to Mutaz Essa Barshim of Qatar, in a red and white 'QATAR' uniform, who also successfully clears the 2.20m bar with a Fosbury Flop, celebrating with a raised arm before walking off. Next, Bohdan Bondarenko of Ukraine, in a blue and yellow uniform with 'UKRAINE' and 'BONDARENKO' on his bib, approaches the bar, clears it with a powerful jump, and lands with a triumphant fist pump. The on-screen graphics display each athlete’s name, country, and jump heights, including 2.20m, 2.25m, and 2.29m attempts. The environment features the green-poled high jump apparatus, Rio 2016 branding, and a vibrant crowd, with officials in blue jackets observing from the sidelines. The atmosphere is intense and celebratory, with each successful jump met with visible satisfaction from the athletes.

segments.caption: The video segment captures a high-intensity sequence from the men's high jump final at the Rio 2016 Olympics, showcasing multiple athletes' attempts at the 2.20m bar. It begins with Bohdan Bondarenko of Ukraine, in a blue and yellow uniform, successfully clearing the bar with a Fosbury Flop, landing on the blue mat with a satisfied expression. The scene transitions to Derek Drouin of Canada, wearing a black and red 'CANADA' uniform with his name and the Canadian flag, who prepares with intense focus, clenching his fist and covering his mouth before executing a clean jump over the bar, landing confidently and walking away with a composed demeanor. The camera then shifts to Mutaz Essa Barshim of Qatar, in a red and white 'QATAR' uniform, who also successfully clears the 2.20m bar with a Fosbury Flop, landing smoothly and walking off with a calm, satisfied look. Throughout, the stadium environment is vibrant, illuminated by bright lights, with spectators filling the blue seats and Rio 2016 branding visible on the landing mat and trackside. On-screen graphics display the athletes' names, nationalities, and jump heights, including 'CAN DEREK DROUIN' and 'QAT MUTAZ ESSA BARSHIM' with their respective 2.20m attempts. A failed attempt by an athlete in a red uniform is briefly shown, knocking the bar off with his back, followed by a disappointed walk away. Trackside officials in blue jackets observe the jumps, and a Qatar flag banner is visible in the background during Barshim’s attempt, adding to the national atmosphere. The sequence highlights the precision, focus, and emotional responses of elite athletes under Olympic pressure.

segments.caption: The video segment captures a high-intensity sequence from the men's high jump event at the Rio 2016 Olympics, set in a brightly lit stadium filled with spectators. The action begins with Mutaz Essa Barshim of Qatar, wearing a red and white 'QATAR' uniform with 'BARSHIM' on his bib, executing a successful Fosbury Flop over the 2.20m bar, landing cleanly on the blue mat. He walks away with a composed, satisfied expression, and the on-screen graphic confirms his successful attempt at 2.20m, 2.25m, and 2.29m, all with zero failures. The scene then shifts to Derek Drouin of Canada, in a black and red 'CANADA' uniform with 'DROUIN' and a Canadian flag patch, who also clears the 2.20m bar with a smooth Fosbury Flop, followed by a confident walk and a celebratory gesture. The crowd, including Canadian fans waving a large Canadian flag, erupts in cheers. Drouin’s on-screen graphic shows successful attempts at 2.20m, 2.25m, 2.29m, and 2.33m, with a new attempt at 2.36m. The camera then cuts to Barshim again, who attempts a higher jump but knocks the bar off with his back, resulting in a failed attempt. He walks away with a disappointed expression. The segment ends with a brief shot of a Ukrainian athlete, Bohdan Bondarenko, in a blue and yellow uniform, who successfully clears the bar, followed by a close-up of a Ukrainian official in a blue jacket with a yellow trim and a beanie, reacting with intensity. The High Jump Apparatus, with green poles and a white bar over a blue mat, remains central throughout, while the Stadium Environment, with Rio 2016 branding and vibrant crowd, provides a dynamic backdrop. The Start List Graphics appear for both Barshim and Drouin, displaying their names, national flags, and attempt records. The World and Olympic Record Graphics are not visible in this segment.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of enthusiastic spectators, including a man in a red shirt and lanyard, capturing the moment on his phone, setting a vibrant and engaged atmosphere in the Rio 2016 Olympic stadium. The focus shifts to Mutaz Essa Barshim of Qatar, wearing a red and white 'QATAR' uniform with 'BARSHIM' on his bib, who executes a powerful run-up and successfully clears the 2.20m bar with a Fosbury Flop, landing on the blue mat with a triumphant expression. He then attempts a higher jump, but knocks the bar off with his back, resulting in a failed attempt, and walks away with a disappointed look. The scene transitions to Bohdan Bondarenko of Ukraine, in a blue and yellow 'UKRAINE' uniform, who successfully clears the bar with a smooth Fosbury Flop, landing confidently and showing satisfaction. Next, Derek Drouin of Canada, in a black and red 'CANADA' uniform with 'DROUIN' on his bib, runs up and clears the bar with precision, landing on the mat and celebrating with a raised fist. The video captures the intense focus of the athletes, the reactions of officials and fans, and the dynamic lighting of the stadium at night, with the Rio 2016 branding visible on the landing mat and trackside. The Canadian fan group is seen waving a large Canadian flag and cheering enthusiastically during Drouin’s jump, adding to the electric atmosphere. The on-screen graphics display the athletes’ names, nationalities, and attempt records, including Barshim’s 2.20m, 2.25m, 2.29m, and 2.33m attempts, Bondarenko’s 2.20m, 2.25m, 2.29m, 2.33m, and 2.38m attempts, and Drouin’s 2.20m, 2.25m, 2.29m, 2.33m, and 2.36m attempts, all contributing to the competitive narrative of the high jump event.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with Bohdan Bondarenko of Ukraine, wearing a blue and yellow uniform with 'UKRAINE' and 'BONDARENKO' on his bib, walking confidently across the blue track toward the high jump area, with photographers and spectators in the background. The camera briefly focuses on a red OMEGA timer displaying '0:25' and '0:29', indicating the time remaining for the athlete's attempt. Bondarenko then stands at the start of the runway, hands on hips, preparing for his jump, while the crowd watches intently. He begins his run-up with focused determination, accelerating toward the green-poled high jump apparatus. As he approaches, he executes a Fosbury Flop, clearing the bar at 2.38m with precision and landing safely on the blue mat. Officials in blue jackets adjust the bar, and the on-screen graphic updates his score to 'O' for a successful clearance. The scene then cuts to Derek Drouin of Canada, wearing a black and red uniform with 'CANADA' and 'DROUIN' on his bib, standing with hands on hips, looking focused and composed. He then runs down the track with intensity, preparing for his own attempt. The stadium environment is brightly lit at night, with Rio 2016 branding visible on the landing mat and banners, and spectators in the stands waving flags, including a large Canadian flag. The atmosphere is charged with anticipation as the athletes prepare for their respective jumps.

segments.caption: The video segment captures the climax of the Men's High Jump final at the Rio 2016 Olympics, focusing on the final results and athlete reactions. The sequence begins with Derek Drouin of Canada, wearing his black and red 'CANADA' uniform with a Canadian flag patch, celebrating his successful 2.38m jump with arms raised in triumph, as the on-screen graphic confirms his victory. The camera then cuts to the Canadian fan group in the stands, waving flags and cheering enthusiastically, many wearing red and white Canada apparel and hats. The scene shifts to Mutaz Essa Barshim of Qatar, dressed in a red jacket and matching cap with a white winged logo, who is shown smiling and raising his arms in celebration after securing second place with a 2.36m jump. The on-screen graphic confirms his 2nd place finish. Next, Bohdan Bondarenko of Ukraine, in his blue and yellow uniform, is seen stretching and preparing to leave the competition area after finishing third with a 2.33m jump, as the graphic displays his result. The stadium environment remains vibrant, illuminated by bright lights, with the Rio 2016 branding visible on the landing mat and trackside. The OMEGA timer and official scoreboards are visible, reinforcing the competitive atmosphere. The segment concludes with a wide shot of the stadium, capturing the celebratory mood and the athletes' post-competition interactions.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with a close-up of an athlete in a red uniform kneeling on the blue landing mat, gathering his belongings, including a red cap and a white towel, while a photographer in a green shirt kneels nearby capturing the moment. The scene transitions to a wide shot of the stadium, where the official results for the Men's High Jump Final are displayed on a large on-screen graphic. The scoreboard shows Derek Drouin of Canada in first place with 2.38m, Mutaz Essa Barshim of Qatar in second with 2.36m, and Bohdan Bondarenko of Ukraine in third with 2.33m. The graphic scrolls to reveal additional competitors, including Robert Grabarz (GBR), Andriy Protsenko (UKR), Erik Kynard (USA), Majd Eddin Ghazal (SYR), Kyriakos Ioannou (CYP), Donald Thomas (BAH), Tikhomir Ivanov (BUL), Trevor Barry (BAH), Dimitrios Chondrokoukis (CYP), Luis Joel Castro (PUR), Jaroslav Baba (CZE), and Brandon Starc (AUS), with their respective jump heights. The camera then pans across the empty high jump area, showing the green-poled apparatus and the blue 'Rio 2016' landing mat, as officials and staff walk around the track. The final shot shows a man in a red shirt and black pants, likely a volunteer or official, walking past white benches and green recycling bins near the track, with the stadium lights illuminating the night sky and the crowd visible in the background.
